config.description: Dissertation D08 as a coordinated T1 controller launcher with AP-B UDP sensing, derived feature/controller sidecars, and protected-traffic sidecars

sensor.label: node-a
sensor.public_command_role: primary AP UDP summary capture command

capture_blocks.objective: confirm that sensing, protected-traffic, and controller sidecars are all active before the first measured block
collect the empty baseline for the controller surface
capture_blocks.scenario: shared empty-scene reference under moderate protected traffic

capture_blocks.objective: collect the first controller-driven static segment
capture_blocks.scenario: static presence in the shared interaction zone

capture_blocks.objective: collect the first controller-driven motion segment
capture_blocks.scenario: slow motion through the shared interaction zone

capture_blocks.objective: observe the reverse transition while traffic remains active
capture_blocks.scenario: empty recovery after the first cycle

capture_blocks.objective: provide an in-session repeat for the controller comparison
provide an in-session repeat for the controller comparison
complete the controller bundle and notes

honesty_notes: D08 combines cooperative sensing with controller and protected-traffic artifacts.
Controller decisions were derived from live node A/B feature sidecars; if those inputs had failed or emitted only parse errors, the run would not be claim-grade.
Completed D08 requires controller decisions and protected-traffic traces to overlap on the same block timeline and to retain at least one real throughput sample.
The dedicated Node-C helper reused the same two-Wi-Fi AP path family introduced for D07, but public metadata redacts helper addresses, SSIDs, and interface names.
Node B uses the same real AP-B UDP summary path that made D05-D07 claim-grade; the RPi4 UART path remains diagnostic only unless separately validated.
Protected traffic was generated on the Node-C helper path as live, non-emulated traffic for the current stand.
The Node-C ESP32-S3 corner placement remained documented physical context only and was not promoted to a live D08 sensing-node claim.

operator_notes: D08 reuses the T1 cooperative stand but adds controller and protected-traffic artifacts.

Use the corrected D07-D09 clock-face layout: Node A ESP32-C5 connected to the laptop by USB at 12 o'clock / 0 degrees; primary router/AP at 6 o'clock / 180 degrees; Node B RPi5 at 9 o'clock / 270 degrees; Node B RPi4 with USB-connected ESP32-C5 at 3 o'clock / 90 degrees; Node C RPi5 at 7:30 / 7.5 o'clock / 225 degrees; Node C ESP32-S3 power-only receiver off the 1.00 m ring at 1:30 / 1.5 o'clock / 45 degrees, 2.00 m from the experiment center.
Clock-to-degree convention: 12 o'clock is 0 degrees, 3 o'clock is 90 degrees, 6 o'clock is 180 degrees, and 9 o'clock is 270 degrees, measured clockwise from 12.

Primary ring radius: Node A ESP32-C5, router/AP, Node B RPi5, Node B RPi4 plus ESP32-C5, and Node C RPi5 are each 1.00 m from the experiment center; the Node C ESP32-S3 is the only off-ring device and sits in the separate 1:30 corner at 2.00 m from the center.
Controlled primary sensing diameter: Node A ESP32-C5 at 12 o'clock / 0 degrees to the router/AP at 6 o'clock / 180 degrees, for a 2.00 m line through the experiment center.

Router/AP, active esp32-c5-a, rpi5-a, rpi4-a, Node-C RPi5, and Node-C ESP32-S3 stay at 0.50 m height unless otherwise recorded; the capture laptop stays adjacent to the Node A USB setup and must not intrude into another sensing corridor.

Node A is captured over the primary-router UDP DNAT listener on the laptop; <local-serial-device> is used for liveness/reset and serial drain, not as the claim-grade data transport for the current firmware image.
Node B is the separate AP-B plane and is expected to stream real UDP CSI summaries from esp32-c5-b through the helper AP path.

Protected traffic comes from the dedicated Node-C helper path between the helper RPi5 and the laptop-side second Wi-Fi path; public metadata redacts the private addresses and interface names used during the run.
Controller decisions were derived from live feature-window sidecars instead of an emulated stream.
Keep the helper, traffic, and management paths out of band and do not let them contaminate the 12-to-6 sensing corridor.

Post-run public status: the D08 controller, protected-traffic, and timing evidence remained live and synchronized, but the raw sidecar artifacts are intentionally excluded from this public repository.
topology_notes: D07-D09 clock/degree convention: 12 o'clock = 0 degrees, 3 o'clock = 90 degrees, 6 o'clock = 180 degrees, 9 o'clock = 270 degrees, measured clockwise from 12.

Primary 1.00 m ring around the experiment center: Node A ESP32-C5 connected to the laptop by USB at 12 o'clock / 0 degrees; primary router/AP at 6 o'clock / 180 degrees; Node B RPi5 at 9 o'clock / 270 degrees; Node B RPi4 with USB-connected ESP32-C5 at 3 o'clock / 90 degrees; Node C RPi5 at 7:30 / 7.5 o'clock / 225 degrees, midway along the perimeter between the router/AP and the Node B RPi5.
Off-ring Node C receiver: ESP32-S3 is connected only to power in the separate 1:30 / 1.5 o'clock / 45 degrees corner, 2.00 m from the experiment center.
Controlled primary sensing diameter: Node A ESP32-C5 at 12 o'clock / 0 degrees to the router/AP at 6 o'clock / 180 degrees.
Vertical placement: router/AP, active esp32-c5-a, rpi5-a, rpi4-a, Node-C RPi5, and Node-C ESP32-S3 at 0.50 m unless otherwise recorded; capture laptop adjacent to the Node A USB setup.
Node A is live through the primary-router UDP DNAT summary path, and Node B is live through the AP-B UDP summary path.
The controller layer remained aligned to the same shared block-event timeline and cooperative timing evidence on top of this stand.
Protected traffic belongs to the dedicated Node-C helper path and not to the router/AP sensing plane.
For D08, the Node-C ESP32-S3 power-only corner placement is documented as physical context only and is not promoted to a sensing-node claim.
Protected traffic and controller outputs must remain aligned with the sensing blocks and final notes must mark whether any traffic or controller artifact was real or emulated.

checkpoints.description: controller-side mid-session alignment checkpoint before empty recovery

ring_devices.device: Node A ESP32-C5 connected to laptop by USB
Node B RPi4 with USB-connected ESP32-C5

off_ring_devices.device: Node C ESP32-S3 connected only to power

physical_topology.primary_sensing_diameter: Node A ESP32-C5 at 12 oclock / 0 degrees to primary AP at 6 oclock / 180 degrees

sanitization_summary: removed absolute local paths, private helper-host addresses, AP credentials, adapter names, and runtime-specific environment details from metadata and notes
kept both raw sensing capture logs and the operator/timing event logs for auditability
preserved the claim-grade D08 controller and protected-traffic result while keeping raw sidecar outputs out of the public repository
